title: First federally approved US monument to LGBT veterans dedicated in Illinois
description: A monument honoring LGBT veterans was dedicated at Abraham Lincoln National Cemetery in Elwood, Illinois, the first such memorial to receive US federal approval.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stonewall Inn designated a New York City landmark for its role in gay rights
description: The New York City Landmarks Preservation Commission voted unanimously to designate the Stonewall Inn a city landmark, the first site in the city protected solely for its LGBT cultural significance. The commission had announced its consideration of the site on 29 May 2015.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Royal Vauxhall Tavern becomes first UK building listed for its LGBT history
description: The UK Department of Culture, Media and Sport granted Grade II listed status to the Royal Vauxhall Tavern in London, the first building in the country protected on the basis of its LGBT heritage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bisexual website Biscuit launches the Purple List
description: The UK bisexual women's website Biscuit created the Purple List, the first known ranking recognizing people who worked against biphobia and raised bisexual visibility.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jacob Anderson-Minshall is first openly transgender author to win a Goldie award
description: Jacob Anderson-Minshall won a Goldie award from the Golden Crown Literary Society, the first openly transgender author to do so, sharing the best creative non-fiction prize with Diane Anderson-Minshall for Queerly Beloved: A Love Story Across Genders.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Audrey Middleton is Big Brother's first openly transgender houseguest
description: Audrey Middleton appeared on the US television show Big Brother as the program's first openly transgender houseguest.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly transgender actor in a major US daytime television role
description: Scott Turner Schofield played Nick on The Bold and the Beautiful, the first major daytime television role held by an openly transgender actor. On the same show, Maya Avant (Karla Mosley) became daytime television's first transgender bride when the character married Rick Forrester.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hari Nef is first openly transgender model signed to IMG
description: Model and actress Hari Nef signed with IMG, the first openly transgender model on the agency's roster.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andrew Guy becomes Australia's first openly transgender TV host
description: Andrew Guy appeared as a guest presenter on The Project, making him the first openly transgender television host in Australia.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Scottish LGBTI Awards held
description: Scotland held its first LGBTI Awards ceremony, recognizing contributions to LGBTI life in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Neil Patrick Harris is first openly gay man to host the Academy Awards
description: Actor Neil Patrick Harris hosted the Academy Awards ceremony, the first openly gay man to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First producer-backed Oscar campaigns for openly transgender actresses
description: The producers of the film Tangerine launched Oscar campaigns for actresses Kitana Kiki Rodriguez and Mya Taylor, the first such campaigns backed by a movie producer for openly transgender performers.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: EastEnders casts first openly transgender actor in a UK TV soap
description: EastEnders cast Riley Carter Millington as Kyle, a trans man, making him the first openly transgender actor in the history of British television soaps. Hollyoaks cast transgender actress Annie Wallace shortly afterward.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Inga Beale tops the OUTstanding & FT LGBT executive power list
description: Lloyd's of London CEO Inga Beale was ranked number one on the OUTstanding & FT Leading LGBT executive power list, the first woman and first openly bisexual person to head the ranking.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Loiza Lamers is first openly transgender winner of a Top Model franchise
description: Loiza Lamers won Holland's Next Top Model, the first openly transgender contestant to win any edition of the international Top Model franchise.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mya Taylor is first openly transgender actress to win a Gotham Award
description: Mya Taylor won the Gotham Award for Breakthrough Actor for her role in Tangerine, becoming the first openly transgender actress honored with a Gotham Award.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Denise Eger is first openly gay president of the Central Conference of American Rabbis
description: Rabbi Denise Eger took office as president of the Central Conference of American Rabbis, North America's largest and oldest rabbinical organization, its first openly gay leader.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Abby Stein comes out as the first openly transgender woman with ultra-Orthodox ordination
description: Abby Stein came out as transgender, making her the first openly transgender woman ordained by an ultra-Orthodox institution; she had received her rabbinical degree from Yeshiva Viznitz in South Fallsburg, New York, in 2011. She is also the first openly transgender woman raised in a Hasidic community.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First male same-sex couple shares a US Navy homecoming first kiss
description: Thomas Sawicki and Shawn Brier were chosen for the traditional first kiss at a US Navy ship's homecoming, the first male same-sex couple selected for the honor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex wedding held in a UK prison
description: Mikhail Ivan Gallatinov and Mark Goodwin married at Full Sutton Prison in East Yorkshire, the first same-sex wedding to take place in a British prison.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Argentina allows same-sex parents plus biological father on a birth certificate
description: Argentina became the first country in Latin America to record same-sex partners together with a biological parent on a child's birth certificate, listing a lesbian couple, Susana Guichal and Valeria Gaete, alongside sperm donor Hernan Melazzi as parents of their son Antonio.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Obama affirms gender identity is no bar to fostering or adoption
description: In his 2015 National Foster Care Month proclamation, President Barack Obama became the first US president to state explicitly that gender identity should not prevent anyone from adopting or serving as a foster parent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First openly gay couple graduates together from a US police academy
description: Shawn MacIver and James Moccia graduated together from the Boston Police Academy, the first openly gay couple known to complete a police academy together.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tokyo's Shibuya ward is first in Japan to certify same-sex partnerships
description: The Shibuya ward of Tokyo passed an ordinance granting same-sex couples partnership certificates, the first jurisdiction in Japan — or anywhere in East Asia — to recognize same-sex partnerships.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ireland legalizes same-sex marriage by popular vote
description: Irish voters approved same-sex marriage in a national referendum, making Ireland the first country to legalize it by popular vote.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Army approves hormone therapy for Chelsea Manning
description: The commandant of Fort Leavenworth approved adding hormone treatment to imprisoned soldier Chelsea Manning's medical plan, a first for the US Army, in a memo dated 5 February 2015 and reported by USA Today the following week.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Federal judge orders California to provide gender-affirming surgery to a prisoner
description: A federal judge ordered California's Department of Corrections to grant transgender prisoner Michelle-Lael Norsworthy access to gender-affirming surgery, a first for the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: White House holds its first Trans Women of Color briefing
description: The inaugural White House Trans Women of Color Women's History Month Briefing took place, bringing transgender women of color to the White House for a dedicated policy event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Justice Department files its first civil lawsuit on behalf of a transgender person
description: The US Justice Department filed United States of America v. Southeastern Oklahoma State University in federal court in Oklahoma on behalf of Rachel Tudor, its first civil lawsuit brought for a transgender plaintiff.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Utah crowns its first openly transgender prom queen
description: Maka Brown, an 18-year-old senior at the Salt Lake School for Performing Arts, was crowned prom queen, the first openly transgender student to receive the title in Utah.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Schools In Transition guide for supporting transgender students published
description: Schools In Transition: A Guide for Supporting Transgender Students in K-12 Schools was released, the first publication of its kind advising school staff and parents on safe and supportive environments for transgender pupils.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Philadelphia flies the transgender pride flag over City Hall
description: Philadelphia raised the transgender pride flag above City Hall for the first time in the city's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: India's first openly transgender college principal takes office
description: Manabi Bandopadhyay began work as principal of Krishnagar Women's College in Nadia district, West Bengal, the first openly transgender person to head a college in India.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Channel 4 documentary shows a phalloplasty on camera for the first time
description: The Channel 4 documentary Girls to Men broadcast a transgender man's phalloplasty, the first time the surgery had been shown on camera.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First US congressional forum on anti-transgender violence
description: The US Congress held its first forum dedicated to violence against transgender people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Department of Veterans Affairs opens its first transgender clinic
description: The US Department of Veterans Affairs opened its first clinic dedicated to transgender service members.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Aydian Dowling is first openly transgender man on a Men's Health cover
description: Aydian Dowling appeared on the cover of a special collector's edition of Men's Health, the first openly transgender man featured on the magazine's cover.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Malta is first country to ban surgery on intersex people without consent
description: Malta outlawed sterilization and invasive surgery on intersex people, the first country in the world to legislate such protections.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Michael Sam signs with the Montreal Alouettes as the CFL's first openly gay player
description: Michael Sam signed a two-year contract with the Montreal Alouettes, becoming the first openly gay player in Canadian Football League history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Michael Sam plays his first CFL regular season game
description: Michael Sam made his Canadian Football League debut for the Montreal Alouettes against the Ottawa Redblacks, the first publicly gay player to appear in a CFL regular season game.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Mosier is first known out trans athlete on a US national team matching his gender
description: Chris Mosier earned a place on Team USA in the men's sprint duathlon, the first known openly transgender athlete to join a US national team matching his gender identity rather than his sex assigned at birth.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sean Conroy is first openly gay player in a professional baseball game
description: Pitcher Sean Conroy appeared in a professional baseball game as an openly gay player, throwing nine scoreless innings for the Sonoma Stompers in a 7-0 win over the Vallejo Admirals in California's independent Pacific Association.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Benjamin Thomas Watt becomes first openly gay professional boxing judge
description: New Zealander Benjamin Thomas Watt became the first openly gay judge in professional boxing.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: David Denson is first active openly gay player in affiliated minor league baseball
description: David Denson came out as gay, the first active minor league player affiliated with a Major League Baseball organization to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Keegan Hirst is first British rugby league professional to come out as gay
description: Rugby league player Keegan Hirst came out as gay, the first British professional in the sport to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sam Stanley is first English rugby union player to come out as gay
description: Sam Stanley, nephew of All Black Joe Stanley, came out as gay, the first English rugby union player to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chris Burns is first openly gay coach in Division I men's basketball
description: Bryant University assistant coach Chris Burns came out as gay, becoming the first openly gay coach in NCAA Division I men's basketball.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Breanna Sinclairé sings the anthem at a pro sports event as an openly trans woman
description: Singer Breanna Sinclairé performed the national anthem at a Major League Baseball game, the first openly transgender person to sing it at a professional sporting event.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Phuti Lekoloane becomes South Africa's first openly gay male footballer
description: Goalkeeper Phuti Lekoloane came out as gay, making him the first openly gay male football player in South Africa.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Adrianna Vorderbruggen killed in combat
description: US Air Force officer Adrianna Vorderbruggen died in combat, believed to be the first openly gay American female active-duty service member and the first openly gay US Air Force officer to be killed in action.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
